question: A company runs a NetApp storage array in an on-premises data center. The company wants to  migrate the storage array to Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP. The company has a mix of NFS  and SMB file shares with complex directory structures and over 60 million small files. The  company has 10 Gbps of network bandwidth available. The company wants to optimize migration                                                                                     Get Latest & Actual SAA-C03 Exam's Question and Answers from Lead2pass.                                  https://www.lead2pass.com   432  efficiency for the file system. Which solution will meet these requirements in the LEAST amount  of time?

options.A: Use AWS DataSync with a bandwidth throttle. Use the All tiering policy.
options.B: Provision an AWS Storage Gateway Volume Gateway. Configure a zero-ETL integration with the  FSx for NetApp ONTAP file system.
options.C: Set up NetApp SnapMirror replication between the on-premises array and the FSx for ONTAP file  system.
options.D: Use AWS Snowball Edge to perform an offline migration.

explanation: NetApp SnapMirror provides native replication from the on-premises NetApp array to Amazon  FSx for NetApp ONTAP. It efficiently handles large numbers of files and complex directory  structures over high-speed connections, minimizing migration time compared to file-based tools.

question: A company uses an organization in AWS Organizations to manage multiple AWS accounts. The  company wants to automatically gather compliance artifacts for resources that are not compliant  with security best practices. Which solution will meet this requirement with the LEAST operational  overhead?

options.A: Create an AWS Lambda function in each AWS account to collect compliance artifacts. Send the  results to an Amazon S3 bucket in a central account.
options.B: Configure AWS Audit Manager and Integrate with AWS Organizations. Use Audit Manager to  collect compliance artifacts in a designated management account. Use Audit Manager evidence  finder in the management account to collect compliance violations from all accounts.
options.C: Configure AWS Control Tower. Extend governance to the organization. Use Control Tower to set  compliance guardrails for all AWS accounts in the organization. Create AWS Lambda functions in  each account to collect guardrail violations and to send the violations to an Amazon S3 bucket in  a central account.
options.D: Configure Amazon GuardDuty and integrate with AWS Organizations. Use GuardDuty to collect  compliance artifacts in a designated GuardDuty management account.

explanation: AWS Audit Manager integrated with AWS Organizations automatically collects compliance  artifacts across all accounts with minimal operational overhead. The management account  centralizes evidence collection, and the evidence finder feature enables identification of  noncompliant resources efficiently.

question: A company is creating a payment processing application that supports TLS connections from  IPv4 clients. The application requires outbound access to the public internet. The application  must allow users to access the application from a single entry point while maintaining the lowest  possible attack surface.    The company wants to use Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) tasks to deploy the  application. The company wants to enable awsvpc network mode.    Which solution will meet these requirements?                                                                                       Get Latest & Actual SAA-C03 Exam's Question and Answers from Lead2pass.                                  https://www.lead2pass.com   433

options.A: Create a VPC that has an internet gateway, public subnets, and private subnets. Deploy a  Network Load Balancer (NLB) and a NAT gateway in the public subnets. Deploy the ECS tasks in  the private subnets.
options.B: Create a VPC that has an outbound-only internet gateway, public subnets, and private subnets.  Deploy an Application Load Balancer (ALB) and a NAT gateway in the public subnets. Deploy the  ECS tasks in the private subnets.
options.C: Create a VPC that has an internet gateway, public subnets, and private subnets. Deploy an  Application Load Balancer (ALB) in the public subnets. Deploy the ECS tasks in the public  subnets.
options.D: Create a VPC that has an outbound-only internet gateway, public subnets, and private subnets.  Deploy a Network Load Balancer (NLB) in the public subnets. Deploy the ECS tasks in the public  subnets.

explanation: Deploying ECS tasks in public subnets with awsvpc network mode allows each task to have its  own elastic network interface and direct internet access for outbound connections. Placing an  Application Load Balancer in the public subnets provides a single entry point for users, minimizing  the attack surface while supporting TLS connections.

question: An advertising company stores terabytes of data in an Amazon S3 data lake. The company wants  to build its own foundation model (FM) and has deployed a training cluster on AWS. The  company loads file-based data from Amazon S3 to the training cluster to train the FM. The  company wants to reduce data loading time to optimize the overall deployment cycle.    The company needs a storage solution that is natively integrated with Amazon S3. The solution  must be scalable and provide high throughput.    Which storage solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Mount an Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) file system to the training cluster. Use AWS  DataSync to migrate data from Amazon S3 to the EFS file system to train the FM.
options.B: Use an Amazon FSx for Lustre file system and Amazon S3 with Data Repository Association  (DRA). Preload the data from Amazon S3 to the Lustre file system to train the FM.
options.C: Attach Amazon Block Store (Amazon EBS) volumes to the training cluster. Load the data from  Amazon S3 to the EBS volumes to train the FM.
options.D: Use AWS DataSync to migrate the data from Amazon S3 to the training cluster as files. Train the  FM on the local file-based data.

explanation: Amazon FSx for Lustre natively integrates with Amazon S3 via a Data Repository Association,  providing a high-throughput, scalable file system. Preloading S3 data into FSx for Lustre allows  the training cluster to access data quickly, reducing data loading time and optimizing the  foundation model training cycle.

question: A company runs an application that consists of multiple microservices. The company mandates  that the microservices use messages to communicate with each other. The application must  archive the microservices’ messages for 30 days. Which solution will meet these requirements  with the LEAST amount of development effort?                                                                                       Get Latest & Actual SAA-C03 Exam's Question and Answers from Lead2pass.                                  https://www.lead2pass.com   434

options.A: Use an Amazon EventBridge event bus to route messages between the microservices. Use  message filtering to ensure that each microservice receives only messages that are relevant to  each microservice. Create an archive in Amazon EventBridge to store the messages for 30 days.
options.B: Use a single Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic to distribute the messages.  Subscribe each microservice to the topic. Use message filtering to ensure that each microservice  receives only messages that are relevant to each microservice. Use an Amazon Simple Queue  Service (Amazon SQS) queue to store the messages for 30 days.
options.C: Use a single Amazon Simple Notification Service (Amazon SNS) topic to distribute the messages.  Subscribe each microservice to the topic. Use message filtering to ensure that each microservice  receives only messages that are relevant to each microservice. Store messages in an Amazon  S3 bucket for 30 days.
options.D: Create a private Amazon API Gateway HTTP API for the microservices to send messages. Use  an AWS Lambda function to forward the messages to the appropriate microservices based on  query parameters. Archive messages in Amazon S3 for 30 days.

explanation: Amazon EventBridge provides native message routing with filtering and supports built-in  message archiving for up to 90 days. This allows microservices to receive only relevant  messages while automatically storing all messages for 30 days, minimizing development effort.

question: A global manufacturing company wants to implement disaster recovery for the company’s existing  on-premises production servers. The company needs a solution to replicate the existing servers  without the need to reinstall the operating system or applications. The solution must achieve the  lowest possible recovery time objective (RTO). Which solution will meet these requirements?

options.A: Create scheduled Amazon Machine Image (AMI) backups. Use Amazon EC2 Image Builder to  implement a manual failover process. Configure AWS CloudFormation templates to automate the  disaster recovery process.
options.B: Configure AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery to perform continuous data replication for the existing  instances and to automate the disaster recovery process.
options.C: Configure an AWS Storage Gateway Tape Gateway to create virtual tape backups of on- premises virtual machines (VMs). Store the VM backups in Amazon S3. Manually recover the  VMs during disaster scenarios.
options.D: Use AWS Application Migration Service to replicate the data from on-premises servers to AWS.  Perform a manual cutover of the instances during disaster scenarios.

explanation: AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery continuously replicates on-premises servers to AWS and  automates failover, enabling near-zero RTO without reinstalling operating systems or  applications. This provides a fast, managed disaster recovery solution with minimal operational  effort.

question: A company is developing a photo-hosting application in the us-east-1 Region. The application  gives users across multiple countries the ability to upload and view photos. Some photos are  heavily viewed for months, while other photos are viewed for less than a week. The application  allows users to upload photos that are up to 20 MB in size. The application uses photo metadata  to determine which photos to display to each user. The company needs a cost-effective storage  solution to support the application. Which solution will meet this requirement?                                                                                       Get Latest & Actual SAA-C03 Exam's Question and Answers from Lead2pass.                                  https://www.lead2pass.com   435

options.A: Store the photos in Amazon DynamoDB. Turn on DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX).
options.B: Store the photos in the Amazon S3 Intelligent-Tiering storage class. Store the photo metadata  and the S3 location URLs in Amazon DynamoDB.
options.C: Store the photos in the Amazon S3 Standard storage class. Set up an S3 Lifecycle policy to move  photos older than 30 days to the S3 Standard-Infrequent Access (S3 Standard-IA) storage class.  Use object tags to keep track of metadata.
options.D: Store the photos in an Amazon DynamoDB table. Use the DynamoDB Standard-Infrequent  Access (DynamoDB Standard-IA) storage class. Store the photo metadata in Amazon  ElastiCache.

explanation: Storing photos in Amazon S3 Intelligent-Tiering automatically moves objects between frequent  and infrequent access tiers based on access patterns, optimizing cost for photos with variable  popularity. Using DynamoDB to store metadata and S3 object URLs provides fast retrieval of  photo information without incurring high storage costs.
